description: In Go, when go.mod / go.sum, the module graph, and indirect requirements change, verify the lockfile, generated artifacts, and a runtime smoke.
body: In Go, a dependency change ripples through go.mod / go.sum, the module graph, and indirect requirements, generated bindings, and runtime behavior, so it needs more than a recompile. Use when go.mod / go.sum, the module graph, and indirect requirements are added, removed, or version-bumped. Do not merge a dependency bump on a green compile alone, and avoid editing the lockfile by hand. Actions: inspect the dependency delta, re-resolve and review go.mod / go.sum, the module graph, and indirect requirements, regenerate affected go generate output and code emitted by stringer/protoc-gen-go, then run go test ./... -race on the changed package and smoke the import/runtime path. Validation signals include a reviewed lockfile diff, a clean go vet, staticcheck, and the race detector (go test -race) pass, and a passing runtime smoke. Risk boundary: escalate on a major-version bump, a transitive conflict you cannot resolve, or a license/security flag.
